title: Lotka-Volterra Predator-Prey Dynamics
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Lotka-Volterra Predator-Prey Dynamics: Lotka-Volterra parameter estimation: infer growth rates and interaction coefficients from time-series population data. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.ode.lotka_volterra…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~20; stochastic_demographic_noise, environmental_variability set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: dH/dt = r*H - a*H*P; dP/dt = b*a*H*P - m*P; neutrally stable oscillations
E.observation_y: population counts {H(t_i), P(t_i)} from census or survey data

W.regime: Existence of the recovered LV_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 20); stochastic_demographic_noise dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Lognormal sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: log-population RMSE < 0.1 for well-sampled data. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~20. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |stochastic_demographic_noise|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_timepoints * N_params) per ODE + fit per iteration
